primary_variables.matched_definition: Intervention using a compound that inhibits a target protein, pathway, or process.
evidence: we developed VPA-functionalized derivatives of the FDA-approved covalent inhibitors Afatinib and Ibrutinib and evaluated their ability to engage the target protein by gel-based and mass spectrometry-based activity-based protein profiling (ABPP).
we performed a proteomic pulldown experiment. Following a 2 h incubation at 37 °C (1 μM compound, 1% DMSO), cells were washed, lysed, and clicked to biotin-N3

primary_variables.matched_definition: Intervention using a compound that inhibits a target protein, pathway, or process.
evidence: we leveraged the modular nature and accessibility of VPAs to develop a bifunctional proteolysis targeting chimera (PROTAC) for targeted protein degradation.
we then evaluated whether 14 could induce BTK degradation in the RAMOS cells... we investigated the proteome-wide selectivity of 14 by using label-free quantitative proteomics.
Following a 2 h incubation at 37 °C (1 μM compound, 1% DMSO), cells were washed, lysed, and clicked to biotin-N3
Additionally, we also wanted to investigate proteomic engagement under more extreme conditions, using a 10 μM probe concentration.
we performed the experiment with 10 μM of the corresponding probe [VPA 9]... in-cell labeling followed by streptavidin pull-down coupled to LC-MS/MS analysis.
Western blot analysis indicated a concentration-dependent degradation of BTK after 24 h
we investigated the proteome-wide selectivity of 14 by using label-free quantitative proteomics.
LFQ of the proteomic analysis revealed that EGFR is the only protein significantly enriched for 7 compared to the DMSO-treated control
While VPA 9 managed to enrich BTK in a highly selective manner with only three off-target proteins, acrylamide-based probe 12 was by far more promiscuous (73 off-targets).
